title: Enhancing Fault Troubleshooting through Human-Machine Collaboration: A Multi-Stage Reasoning Approach

agents.description: LLM-based agent (GPT-4) responsible for Knowledge Graph Construction tasks including Named Entity Recognition, Relation Extraction, Causal Relation Extraction, and Fault Feature Classification from fault tickets.

Graph embedding agent using BGE-base for node feature initialization and R-GAT for aggregating graph node information to obtain embedding representations, used for retrieving similar fault symptoms from the DSFCKG.

genai_model.finetuning_dataset_description: Contrastive learning dataset of 2000 fault symptom node pairs derived from real-world fault tickets, with 70% training, 15% validation, 15% test split.

orchestration.orchestration_pattern_description: The system follows a multi-stage orchestration graph: first, the LLM-based KGC agent constructs the DSFCKG from historical fault tickets; then, during troubleshooting, the graph embedding retriever recommends fault locations based on the worker's input, and the worker performs on-site investigation and provides feedback, enabling iterative multi-stage reasoning until the root cause is identified.

multi_agent_coordination_strategy.multi_agent_coordination_strategy_descri: The LLM-based KGC agent and the graph embedding retriever cooperate sequentially: the KGC agent builds the knowledge graph offline, and the retriever uses it online during fault troubleshooting to recommend fault locations iteratively in collaboration with human workers.

human_integration.human_integration_description: Workers initiate the troubleshooting process by describing fault phenomena in natural language. The system recommends potential fault locations, and workers conduct on-site inspections and provide feedback on findings. This iterative human-machine collaboration continues through multiple rounds until the root cause is identified.

retrieval.application_domain_description: Fault troubleshooting and diagnosis for critical industrial equipment (specifically bridge cranes in a steel plant), aiming to identify root causes of faults through human-machine collaboration.
retrieval.problem_description: Modern industrial machinery is complex, placing a heavy cognitive burden on maintenance workers. Fault descriptions in natural language are vague and non-standardized, and root causes of faults are often not directly discoverable but require multi-stage reasoning through causal chains. Existing automated fault diagnosis systems are limited to specific fault types and do not handle long fault causal chains effectively.
retrieval.proposed_solution: A Diagnostic Semantics-Enhanced Fault Causality Knowledge Graph (DSFCKG) is proposed to formalize fault information, constructed automatically using an LLM-based (GPT-4) Knowledge Graph Construction method. A graph embedding model (BGE-base + R-GAT) retrieves similar fault cases. A multi-stage reasoning-based human-machine collaborative fault troubleshooting framework iteratively recommends fault locations to workers, who perform on-site inspections and provide feedback, enabling gradual root cause identification.
retrieval.research_methodology_description: The proposed method is validated using 471 real-world fault tickets from a steel plant's bridge crane (2019-2021). A case study demonstrates the multi-stage troubleshooting process, and comparative experiments against CausalKG-ALBERT and traditional CBR methods are conducted on 30 randomly selected fault cases.

decision_quality_improvement: The proposed fault troubleshooting method outperforms CausalKG-ALBERT and traditional CBR in both AccRC (root cause identification ratio) and ScoreTS (troubleshooting efficiency score) metrics across 30 test cases.
The graph embedding method (BGE-base + R-GAT) achieved higher accuracy and lower loss than plain text similarity embedding (BGE-base alone) on the contrastive learning dataset.
The R-GAT-based graph embedding model achieved 0.886 accuracy on validation set and 0.863 on test set.
95.54% of fault ticket extraction results required minimal manual adjustment under few-shot GPT-4 setting.
reliability_metrics: Graph embedding model: 0.886 accuracy on validation set, 0.863 on test set.
95.54% of LLM-based KGC extractions required minimal manual adjustment.

ce_technique_effectiveness.in-context-learning: Few-shot prompting with 3 representative examples achieved 95.54% extraction accuracy with minimal manual adjustment needed.
ce_technique_effectiveness.retrieval-augmented-generation: Graph embedding retrieval using R-GAT outperformed plain text similarity (BGE-base) in fault symptom similarity tasks, enabling more precise fault location recommendations.
ce_technique_effectiveness.chain-of-thought: Multi-stage reasoning approach consistently verifies facts throughout the reasoning process, leading to more accurate root cause identification especially in complex long fault chain scenarios.

failure_modes: For rare fault symptoms, insufficient historical fault information may be available in the knowledge graph, requiring LLM to directly generate supplementary recommendations.
Current fault troubleshooting only supports single-line fault chain reasoning and cannot handle more complex fault scenarios with branching causal structures.
limitations: Fault feature classification standards rely on worker experience and extensive fault ticket analysis; automated fault feature extraction and updating methods are needed.
Current method only handles single-line fault chain reasoning; more complex fault scenarios require deeper fault causality analysis.
Automated fault cause analysis and solution generation are not yet supported.

baseline_methods: BGE-base plain text similarity embedding model
identified_research_gaps: Automated fault feature extraction and updating methods (currently relies on worker experience and extensive fault ticket analysis)
Current fault troubleshooting only considers single-line fault chain reasoning; deeper fault causality analysis needed for more complex fault scenarios
Future research on automated fault cause analysis and solution generation
